keyPoints: Create Derived object with new keyword
Use Base* pointer to point to Derived object
Call show() through pointer - demonstrates dynamic dispatch
vtable (virtual table) is a lookup table used at runtime to resolve function calls
Each class with virtual functions has its own vtable
Slicing occurs when a derived object is passed by value to a base class parameter
Only the Base portion of the object is copied
Virtual function behavior is lost
Solution: Pass by reference or by pointer
Output will be "Drawing Shape" instead of "Drawing Circle"
Without virtual destructor, only base destructor is called
Derived class destructor is never executed
Memory leak occurs for Derived's member variables
Solution: Add 'virtual ~Base() {}' to base class
Virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup of derived class resources

questions.question:
Create a complete program demonstrating runtime polymorphism with an abstract base class and derived classes. Include:

1. An abstract base class 'Animal' with a pure virtual function 'speak()'
2. Two derived classes 'Dog' and 'Cat' that implement speak() differently
3. A function that uses Animal pointer to call speak() on different derived objects
4. Demonstrate proper memory management with virtual destructor

Write the complete code and explain how the program achieves runtime polymorphism.
keyPoints: Pure virtual function: virtual void speak() = 0;
Abstract class cannot be instantiated
Base class pointer can hold derived class addresses
Virtual function binding happens at runtime (late binding)
Always use virtual destructor in polymorphic base class
Delete each pointer in main to avoid memory leaks
